turns.content: I am trying to add rich, colored help text to my CLI application using clap but I cannot figure out how to enable the styling features.

To get rich help text with colors you need to configure the AppBuilder by calling long_about on the builder instance before building your app. You also want to set value_name to customize what appears next to argument placeholders like a count flag, and use before_help or after_help to insert custom sections at specific points in the output.
I read about hide but I am confused if I should add that to my builder configuration or just leave it out since I want to show all arguments.

The hide method is used when you want an argument not to appear in help text at all, so if you do not use hide then your arguments will be displayed normally. You can still combine long_about with value_name and before_help or after_help to make the output look good without hiding anything.

I'm reviewing this CLI tool I helped build and noticed the help output looks plain. How do we add styled help text with colors using clap? The documentation mentions long_about but I can't seem to get it working.

You're looking at the correct concept, but there's a specific trait you need to implement. You should add the Styled trait from clap::builder to your command definition. This trait lets you define custom formatting for help text using the help formatter builder pattern.
Wait, I already have long_about configured but it's still just plain text. Is there something wrong with how I'm setting up the arguments? Shouldn't that automatically trigger colorized output?

That's a common confusion point when moving from Java to Rust systems programming. The long_about field itself doesn't handle styling; you must explicitly configure the help formatter by implementing the Styled trait on your command. Without that trait implementation, clap will just output raw text regardless of what's in long_about.

I've read about the long_about, hide, value_name, before_help, and after_help options in clap but I still don't fully understand how to implement them. I'm building a CLI tool to automate a repetitive task and my help text looks broken.

It sounds like you're struggling with the help formatting options. Are you using the builder pattern or derive macros? If you tell me which approach you are taking, I can give more specific advice on how to use those fields correctly.
I'm using the derive macros and I've tried setting long_about but it's not showing up. I feel like I'm missing something obvious about how these options interact with the command structure.

The issue is likely that you need to configure the app builder explicitly before applying the derive macros if you want fine-grained control over help text. You should create an App instance, then call methods like long_about, hide, value_name, and after_help on it so those attributes actually get applied to your command arguments.
